text.title: Deployment Strategies — Rolling, Blue/Green, Canary
text.summary: K8s rolling update with readiness probes, blue/green label swap, Istio canary with weight limits, and when to use each.

rule.statement: Set maxSurge and maxUnavailable in rollingUpdate strategy; always configure a readinessProbe with initialDelaySeconds calibrated to actual app boot time.
rule.rationale: Without readinessProbe, Kubernetes sends traffic to pods before they are ready, causing 502s. Without maxUnavailable, zero pods may be available during update if replicas are limited.

example.code: strategy: type: RollingUpdate rollingUpdate: maxSurge: 2 maxUnavailable: 1 template: spec: containers: - name: app readinessProbe: httpGet: { path: /health/ready, port: 8080 } initialDelaySeconds: 10 periodSeconds: 5

rule.statement: Blue/green via label swap is appropriate for stateless services only; stateful services with sticky sessions or DB schema changes require extra coordination before traffic switch.
rule.rationale: A label swap is instant but DB migrations or session stores bound to the old version will break if traffic switches before the data plane is migrated.

rule.statement: Start canary at 5% traffic for a minimum observation window (15 min); promote only if SLO burn rate stays below threshold — not just HTTP 200 status.
rule.rationale: A 50% initial canary with HTTP-200-only gate misses latency regressions, increased error rates on specific endpoints, and downstream service degradation.

antipattern.description: Agent sets canary weight to 50% on first deploy.
antipattern.reason: At 50%, a bad deploy impacts half the user base immediately. Start at 5%, observe, then graduate in steps (5% → 25% → 100%).

rule.statement: Run schema migrations in a dedicated job before the deploy job with its own rollback plan; never inline migrations inside the app container startup.
rule.rationale: App container migrations run in parallel across replicas, causing race conditions. A dedicated job with a lock ensures exactly-once execution with a clear rollback point.
